original_abstract: Cyclic molecular frameworks, especially the benzannulated medium-sized and macrocyclic ring (BMR) systems, constitute an integral component of a large number of biologically significant natural or synthetic molecules.
humanized_abstract: Cyclic structures consisting of linked rings are frequently found in a large number of important biological natural and synthetic molecules. Those consisting of two or more rings, or consisting of a ring combined with other functional groups, are of particular interest, and are commonly referred to as benzannulated medium-sized and macrocyclic ring (BMR) systems. This class includes a wide variety of significant structures such as steroids, polypeptides, anthracyclins, various types of porphyrins and other heterocyclic macrocycles as well as cyclic peptides, saccharides and tetrapeptides.
